human_verification.notes: Reviewed Issue #1002 requirements for BPO supply, procurement, availability, vendor-risk, records, boundary, source, evaluation, functionality-map, research-summary, deployment, and commercialization expectations. Confirmed the package remains specification-based and makes no retained-implementation runtime claim.

review_notes: All required abilities for shared, supply-procurement-and-availability, and cross-industry profiles are declared and validated.
All required queue scenario families and BPO domain failure modes are represented and marked as passing.
Package remains specification-based and documents an explicit promotion path for retained-implementation runtime.

specialist_owned_evidence: Control outputs are bounded to evidence-backed BPO service readiness, queue state, capacity, inventory/license status, approved-provider posture, procurement constraints, vendor-risk escalation, and administrative service handoff records.

Source set includes official NAICS/BLS/Census service references, ISO outsourcing guidance, FAR service-contracting and quality-assurance references, NIST supply-chain risk guidance, NARA records requirements, NIST privacy framework, and AICPA SOC 2 service-organization criteria.

Boundary logic explicitly prevents spend approval, supplier substitution, supplier-risk approval, contract amendment, legal signoff, privacy approval, security incident handling, HR/payroll authority, quality release, regulated-process decisions, service-credit authorization, or final client-facing signoff.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; service-family variation, provider substitution authority, regulated subprocess content, and SOW/policy precedence are explicit retrieval or escalation requirements.

Runtime remains `specification-based`; retained-implementation promotion requires separate governance approval, usage evidence, telemetry review, human-reviewed evidence quality, and a follow-on committed build.
